html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Eighteen — Part Four

The Book of Yirmeyahu

YIRMEYAHU

The Book of Jeremiah — DSS Shorter Recension Primary

Chapters 24 through 31

The Two Baskets of Figs — The Cup of Wrath — The Yoke of Babylon — The Letter to the Exiles — The New Covenant

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJer-a and 4QJer-b — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTERS 24 THROUGH 31 — THE BOOK OF CONSOLATION

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 24 The Two Baskets of Figs — Good Figs and Bad Figs — I Will Give Them a Heart to Know Me

1 After Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon had taken into exile from Yerushalayim Yekhonyahu the son of Yeho'yakim, king of Yehudah, together with the officials of Yehudah, the craftsmen, and the metal workers, and had brought them to Babylon, YHWH showed me this vision: behold, two baskets of figs placed before the temple of YHWH.

2 One basket had very good figs, like first-ripe figs, but the other basket had very bad figs, so bad that they could not be eaten.

3 And YHWH said to me, What do you see, Yirmeyahu? I said, Figs, the good figs very good, and the bad figs very bad, so bad that they cannot be eaten.

4 Then the word of YHWH came to me:

5 Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Like these good figs, so I will regard as good the exiles from Yehudah, whom I have sent away from this place to the land of the Chaldeans.

6 I will set my eyes on them for good, and I will bring them back to this land. I will build them up, and not tear them down. I will plant them, and not uproot them.

7 I will give them a heart to know that I am YHWH, and they shall be my people and I will be their Creator, for they shall return to me with their whole heart.

8 But like the bad figs that are so bad they cannot be eaten, so will I treat Tzidkiyahu the king of Yehudah, his officials, the remnant of Yerushalayim who remain in this land, and those who dwell in the land of Egypt.

9 I will make them a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth, to be a reproach, a byword, a taunt, and a curse in all the places where I shall drive them.

10 And I will send sword, famine, and pestilence upon them, until they shall be utterly destroyed from the land that I gave to them and their fathers.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 24 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The two baskets of figs vision is the covenant theological interpretation of the first deportation of 597 BCE: the exiles taken to Babylon — the bad figs by human assessment — are the good figs in YHWH's covenant economy. Those who remain in Yerushalayim — apparently the survivors, the good figs by human assessment — are the bad figs.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full. Verse 7 — I will give them a heart to know that I am YHWH — anticipates the new covenant declaration of Chapter 31:33-34. The covenant transformation of the heart is YHWH's sovereign gift, not the community's achievement. The exiles receive what the remaining community refuses.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 24. The covenant reversal of verse 5-8 — the exiles are good, the remnant is bad — is the most politically counter-intuitive covenant declaration in the book. Those stripped of land, temple, and king are the covenant future. Those who retain land, temple, and king are the covenant dead end.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — I will give them a heart to know that I am YHWH. The covenant gift of heart-knowledge is the resolution to the diagnosis of Chapter 17:9 — the heart is deceitful above all things. YHWH does not demand a better heart — he gives one. The covenant transformation is unilateral and sovereign. The exiles do not earn the good-fig status — YHWH assigns it and then produces the covenant reality it describes.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 25 Seventy Years of Babylon — The Cup of Wrath — All the Nations Shall Drink — YHWH Roars From on High

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu concerning all the people of Yehudah, in the fourth year of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah — that was the first year of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon —

2 Which Yirmeyahu the prophet spoke to all the people of Yehudah and all the inhabitants of Yerushalayim:

3 For twenty-three years, from the thirteenth year of Yoshiyahu the son of Amon, king of Yehudah, to this day, the word of YHWH has come to me, and I have spoken persistently to you, but you have not listened.

4 You have neither listened nor inclined your ears to hear, although YHWH persistently sent to you all his servants the prophets,

5 Saying, Turn now, every one of you, from his evil way and evil deeds, and dwell upon the land that YHWH has given to you and your fathers from of old and forever.

6 Do not go after other gods to serve and worship them, or provoke me to anger with the work of your hands. Then I will do you no harm.

7 Yet you have not listened to me, declares YHWH, that you might provoke me to anger with the work of your hands to your own harm.

8 Therefore thus says YHWH of hosts: Because you have not obeyed my words,

9 Behold, I will send for all the tribes of the north, declares YHWH, and for Nevukhadnetzar the king of Babylon, my servant, and I will bring them against this land and its inhabitants, and against all these surrounding nations. I will devote them to destruction, and make them a horror, a hissing, and an everlasting desolation.

10 Moreover, I will banish from them the voice of mirth and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the grinding of the millstones and the light of the lamp.

11 This whole land shall become a ruin and a waste, and these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.

12 Then after seventy years are completed, I will punish the king of Babylon and that nation, the land of the Chaldeans, for their iniquity, declares YHWH, making the land an everlasting waste.

13 I will bring upon that land all the words that I have uttered against it, everything written in this scroll, which Yirmeyahu prophesied against all the nations.

14 For many nations and great kings shall make slaves even of them, and I will recompense them according to their deeds and the work of their hands.

15 Thus YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, said to me: Take from my hand this cup of the wine of wrath, and make all the nations to whom I send you drink it.

16 They shall drink and stagger and be crazed because of the sword that I am sending among them.

17 So I took the cup from YHWH's hand, and made all the nations to whom YHWH sent me drink it:

18 Yerushalayim and the cities of Yehudah, its kings and officials, to make them a desolation and a waste, a hissing and a curse, as at this day;

19 Pharaoh king of Egypt, his servants, his officials, all his people,

20 And all the mixed tribes among them; all the kings of the land of Uz; all the kings of the land of the Philistines — Ashkelon, Gaza, Ekron, and the remnant of Ashdod;

21 Edom, Moav, and the sons of Ammon;

22 All the kings of Tzor, all the kings of Tzidon, and the kings of the coastland across the sea;

23 Dedan, Tema, Buz, and all who cut the corners of their hair;

24 All the kings of Arabia and all the kings of the mixed tribes who dwell in the desert;

25 All the kings of Zimri, all the kings of Elam, and all the kings of Media;

26 All the kings of the north, far and near, one after another, and all the kingdoms of the world that are on the face of the earth. And after them the king of Babylon shall drink.

27 Then you shall say to them, Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Drink, be drunk and vomit, fall and rise no more, because of the sword that I am sending among you.

28 And if they refuse to accept the cup from your hand to drink, then you shall say to them, Thus says YHWH of hosts: You must drink.

29 For behold, I begin to work disaster at the city that is called by my name, and shall you go unpunished? You shall not go unpunished, for I am summoning a sword against all the inhabitants of the earth, declares YHWH of hosts.

30 You, therefore, shall prophesy against them all these words, and say to them: YHWH will roar from on high, and from his holy habitation utter his voice. He will roar mightily against his fold, and shout, like those who tread grapes, against all the inhabitants of the earth.

31 The clamor will resound to the ends of the earth, for YHWH has an indictment against the nations. He is entering into judgment with all flesh, and the wicked he will put to the sword, declares YHWH.

32 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Behold, disaster is going forth from nation to nation, and a great tempest is stirring from the farthest parts of the earth.

33 And those pierced by YHWH on that day shall extend from one end of the earth to the other. They shall not be lamented, or gathered, or buried. They shall be dung on the surface of the ground.

34 Wail, you shepherds, and cry out, and roll in ashes, you lords of the flock, for the days of your slaughter and dispersion have come, and you shall fall like a choice vessel.

35 No refuge will remain for the shepherds, nor escape for the lords of the flock.

36 A voice — the cry of the shepherds, and the wail of the lords of the flock. For YHWH is laying waste their pasture,

37 And the peaceful folds are devastated, because of the fierce anger of YHWH.

38 Like a lion he has left his lair, for their land has become a waste because of the sword of the oppressor, and because of his fierce anger.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension of Chapter 25 shows notable variance from the Masoretic. In the DSS and Septuagint, Chapter 25 lacks the extended list of nations found in Masoretic verses 19-26, and the nation oracles that appear in Masoretic Chapters 46-51 appear after Chapter 25 in the Septuagint order. The DSS text follows the shorter Septuagint ordering. The seventy-year prophecy of verse 11-12 is the covenant chronological framework for the entire exile period.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 25 in full. Verse 9 — Nevukhadnetzar the king of Babylon, my servant — is the covenant designation that defines YHWH's sovereign use of pagan kings as covenant instruments of judgment. Nevukhadnetzar is YHWH's servant in the same sense that Assyria was YHWH's rod in Yesha'yahu 10:5.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves a longer form of Chapter 25 with the extended nation list. Verse 15-17 — the cup of YHWH's wrath — is the covenant sign-act that makes global the judgment announced locally against Yehudah. Every nation drinks from the same cup. The covenant judgment of Yehudah is not the end but the beginning of a universal covenant reckoning.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — seventy years. The covenant number that structures Daniyel's later seventy-weeks calculation and shaped the entire Babylonian exile theology. The number is covenant-symbolic: a human lifetime, a Sabbath-of-Sabbaths. Verse 9 — Nevukhadnetzar, my servant. The covenant scandal: YHWH calls the destroyer of Yerushalayim his servant. The covenant sovereignty of YHWH over pagan nations is absolute — he uses them as instruments without endorsing their theology or ethics.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 26 The Temple Sermon Repeated — The Priests and Prophets Seek Yirmeyahu's Death — The Elders Defend Him — Uriyahu the Prophet Slain

1 In the beginning of the reign of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah, this word came from YHWH:

2 Thus says YHWH: Stand in the court of YHWH's house, and speak to all the cities of Yehudah that come to worship in the house of YHWH all the words that I command you to speak to them. Do not hold back a word.

3 It may be they will listen, and every one turn from his evil way, that I may relent of the disaster that I intend to do to them because of their evil deeds.

4 You shall say to them: Thus says YHWH: If you will not listen to me, to walk in my Torah that I have set before you,

5 And to listen to the words of my servants the prophets whom I send to you urgently, though you have not listened,

6 Then I will make this house like Shiloh, and I will make this city a curse for all the nations of the earth.

7 The priests and the prophets and all the people heard Yirmeyahu speaking these words in the house of YHWH.

8 And when Yirmeyahu had finished speaking all that YHWH had commanded him to speak to all the people, then the priests and the prophets and all the people laid hold of him, saying, You shall die.

9 Why have you prophesied in the name of YHWH, saying, This house shall be like Shiloh, and this city shall be desolate, without inhabitant? And all the people gathered around Yirmeyahu in the house of YHWH.

10 When the officials of Yehudah heard these things, they came up from the king's house to the house of YHWH and took their seat in the entry of the New Gate of the house of YHWH.

11 Then the priests and the prophets said to the officials and to all the people: This man deserves the sentence of death, because he has prophesied against this city, as you have heard with your own ears.

12 Then Yirmeyahu spoke to all the officials and all the people, saying: YHWH sent me to prophesy against this house and this city all the words you have heard.

13 Now therefore mend your ways and your deeds, and obey the voice of YHWH your Creator, and YHWH will relent of the disaster that he has pronounced against you.

14 But as for me, behold, I am in your hands. Do with me as seems good and right to you.

15 Only know for certain that if you put me to death, you will bring innocent blood upon yourselves and upon this city and its inhabitants, for in truth YHWH sent me to you to speak all these words in your ears.

16 Then the officials and all the people said to the priests and the prophets: This man does not deserve the sentence of death, for he has spoken to us in the name of YHWH our Creator.

17 And certain of the elders of the land arose and spoke to all the assembled people, saying,

18 Mikhah of Moreshet prophesied in the days of Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah, and said to all the people of Yehudah: Thus says YHWH of hosts: Tziyon shall be plowed as a field. Yerushalayim shall become a heap of ruins, and the mountain of the house a wooded height.

19 Did Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah and all Yehudah put him to death? Did he not fear YHWH and entreat the favor of YHWH, and did not YHWH relent of the disaster that he had pronounced against them? But we are about to bring great disaster upon ourselves.

20 There was another man who prophesied in the name of YHWH, Uriyahu the son of Shemayahu from Kiryat-Ye'arim. He prophesied against this city and against this land in words like all the words of Yirmeyahu.

21 And when King Yeho'yakim, with all his warriors and all the officials, heard his words, the king sought to put him to death. But when Uriyahu heard of it, he was afraid and fled and escaped to Egypt.

22 Then King Yeho'yakim sent to Egypt certain men, Elnatan the son of Akhbor and others with him,

23 And they took Uriyahu from Egypt and brought him to King Yeho'yakim, who struck him down with the sword and dumped his dead body into the burial place of the common people.

24 But the hand of Akhikam the son of Shafan was with Yirmeyahu so that he was not given over to the people to be put to death.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 26 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 26 is the narrative account of the temple sermon of Chapter 7 — the same sermon told from a biographical perspective. The covenant legal proceeding against Yirmeyahu mirrors the covenant lawsuit genre: accusation, defense, verdict. Yirmeyahu's covenant defense is not denial but covenant appeal — verse 12-15.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 26 in full. The account of Uriyahu the prophet — verses 20-23 — is preserved nowhere else in the Hebrew canon. Uriyahu preached the same covenant word as Yirmeyahu but fled when threatened and was executed on return. The covenant contrast with Yirmeyahu is deliberate: the prophet who stands in YHWH's word survives; the prophet who flees does not.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 26. Verse 18 — the elders' defense of Yirmeyahu quotes Mikhah 3:12 verbatim — the only explicit citation of one prophet by another in the Hebrew canon. The covenant precedent is invoked: Mikhah prophesied the same covenant word, Khizkiyahu repented, and YHWH relented. The elders argue that killing the covenant prophet prevents the possibility of the same covenant outcome.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — behold, I am in your hands. Do with me as seems good and right to you. The covenant boldness of Yirmeyahu's defense: he does not appeal for mercy or deny the charge. He places himself entirely in the court's hands while covenant-witnessing that his death would bring innocent blood on the city. The covenant prophet's security is not in the court's verdict but in YHWH's covenant word that sent him.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 27 The Yoke of Babylon — Serve the King of Babylon and Live — Do Not Listen to the False Prophets — The Vessels of the Temple

1 In the beginning of the reign of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah, this word came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH.

2 Thus YHWH said to me: Make yourself straps and yoke-bars, and put them on your neck.

3 Send word to the king of Edom, the king of Moav, the king of the sons of Ammon, the king of Tzor, and the king of Tzidon by the hand of the envoys who have come to Yerushalayim to Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah.

4 Give them this charge for their masters: Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: This is what you shall say to your masters:

5 It is I who by my great power and my outstretched arm have made the earth, with the men and animals that are on the earth, and I give it to whomever it seems right to me.

6 Now I have given all these lands into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar, the king of Babylon, my servant, and I have given him also the beasts of the field to serve him.

7 All the nations shall serve him and his son and his grandson, until the time of his own land comes. Then many nations and great kings shall make him their slave.

8 But if any nation or kingdom will not serve this Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, and put its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon, I will punish that nation with the sword, with famine, and with pestilence, declares YHWH, until I have consumed it by his hand.

9 So do not listen to your prophets, your diviners, your dreamers, your fortune-tellers, or your sorcerers, who are saying to you, You shall not serve the king of Babylon.

10 For it is a lie that they are prophesying to you, with the result that you will be removed far from your land, and I will drive you out, and you will perish.

11 But any nation that will bring its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon and serve him, I will leave on its own land, to work it and dwell there, declares YHWH.

12 To Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah I spoke in like manner: Bring your necks under the yoke of the king of Babylon, and serve him and his people and live.

13 Why will you and your people die by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence, as YHWH has spoken concerning any nation that will not serve the king of Babylon?

14 Do not listen to the words of the prophets who are saying to you, You shall not serve the king of Babylon, for it is a lie that they are prophesying to you.

15 I have not sent them, declares YHWH, but they are prophesying falsely in my name, with the result that I will drive you out and you will perish, you and the prophets who are prophesying to you.

16 Then I spoke to the priests and to all this people, saying, Thus says YHWH: Do not listen to the words of your prophets who are prophesying to you, saying, Behold, the vessels of YHWH's house will now shortly be brought back from Babylon, for it is a lie that they are prophesying to you.

17 Do not listen to them. Serve the king of Babylon and live. Why should this city become a desolation?

18 If they are prophets, and if the word of YHWH is with them, then let them intercede with YHWH of hosts, that the vessels that are left in the house of YHWH, in the house of the king of Yehudah, and in Yerushalayim may not go to Babylon.

19 For thus says YHWH of hosts concerning the pillars, the sea, the stands, and the rest of the vessels that are left in this city,

20 Which Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon did not take away, when he took into exile from Yerushalayim to Babylon Yekhonyahu the son of Yeho'yakim, king of Yehudah, and all the nobles of Yehudah and Yerushalayim —

21 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL, concerning the vessels that are left in the house of YHWH, in the house of the king of Yehudah, and in Yerushalayim:

22 They shall be carried to Babylon and remain there until the day when I visit them, declares YHWH. Then I will bring them back and restore them to this place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 27 with some textual variance. In the DSS and Septuagint, this chapter refers to Tzidkiyahu as king throughout — the Masoretic opening of Yeho'yakim in verse 1 is widely regarded as a scribal error, since the chapter clearly takes place in Tzidkiyahu's reign. The yoke sign-act — Yirmeyahu wearing an ox-yoke — is the most public and politically provocative sign-act in the book.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 27 in full. Verse 6 — I have given all these lands into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar, my servant — is the covenant declaration of YHWH's absolute sovereignty over geopolitical power: the destroyer of Yerushalayim is YHWH's instrument, not YHWH's adversary. To resist Nevukhadnetzar at this moment is to resist YHWH's covenant purpose.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves Chapter 27 with the verse 1 reference to Yeho'yakim which does not match the chapter context. This is one of the clearest cases where the DSS and Septuagint preserve a more coherent text. Verse 22 — they shall be carried to Babylon and remain there until the day when I visit them — the temple vessels as covenant pledge: YHWH will restore them when the covenant restoration comes.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — do not listen to your prophets, your diviners, your dreamers, your fortune-tellers, or your sorcerers. The covenant warning covers every category of religious authority the surrounding nations rely on. All of them are saying the same thing — you shall not serve Babylon — and all of them are wrong. The covenant truth runs counter to every institutional religious voice in the region. Yirmeyahu stands alone against the entire prophetic consensus.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 28 Hananyah the False Prophet — He Breaks the Yoke — YHWH Has Not Sent You — Within This Year You Will Die

1 In that same year, at the beginning of the reign of Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah, in the fifth month of the fourth year, Hananyah the son of Azzur, the prophet from Giv'on, spoke to me in the house of YHWH, in the presence of the priests and all the people, saying,

2 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: I have broken the yoke of the king of Babylon.

3 Within two years I will bring back to this place all the vessels of YHWH's house, which Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon took away from this place and carried to Babylon.

4 I will also bring back to this place Yekhonyahu the son of Yeho'yakim, king of Yehudah, and all the exiles from Yehudah who went to Babylon, declares YHWH, for I will break the yoke of the king of Babylon.

5 Then the prophet Yirmeyahu spoke to Hananyah the prophet in the presence of the priests and all the people who were standing in the house of YHWH,

6 And the prophet Yirmeyahu said, Amen — may YHWH do so. May YHWH make the words that you have prophesied come true, and bring back to this place from Babylon the vessels of the house of YHWH, and all the exiles.

7 Yet hear now this word that I speak in your hearing and in the hearing of all the people.

8 The prophets who preceded you and me from ancient times prophesied war, famine, and pestilence against many countries and great kingdoms.

9 As for the prophet who prophesies peace, when the word of that prophet comes to pass, then it will be known that YHWH has truly sent the prophet.

10 Then the prophet Hananyah took the yoke-bar from the neck of Yirmeyahu the prophet and broke it.

11 And Hananyah spoke in the presence of all the people, saying, Thus says YHWH: Even so will I break the yoke of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon from the neck of all the nations within two years. But Yirmeyahu the prophet went his way.

12 Sometime after the prophet Hananyah had broken the yoke-bar from off the neck of Yirmeyahu the prophet, the word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu:

13 Go, tell Hananyah, Thus says YHWH: You have broken bars of wood, but you have made in their place bars of iron.

14 For thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: I have put upon the neck of all these nations an iron yoke to serve Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, and they shall serve him, for I have given to him even the beasts of the field.

15 And Yirmeyahu the prophet said to the prophet Hananyah, Listen, Hananyah — YHWH has not sent you, and you have made this people trust in a lie.

16 Therefore thus says YHWH: Behold, I will remove you from the face of the earth. This year you shall die, because you have uttered rebellion against YHWH.

17 In that same year, in the seventh month, the prophet Hananyah died.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 28 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The confrontation between Yirmeyahu and Hananyah is the most dramatically concentrated false-prophecy narrative in the entire prophetic corpus. Both men speak in the name of YHWH — both invoke the covenant formula. The external appearance of their prophecy is identical. The difference is authorization: YHWH sent one and not the other.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 28 in full. Verse 6 — Amen, may YHWH do so — is Yirmeyahu's covenant response to Hananyah's prophecy of restoration. The response is not sarcastic but genuinely sorrowful: Yirmeyahu wishes the false prophecy were true. The covenant prophet who announces judgment does not delight in it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 28. Verse 13 — you have broken bars of wood, but you have made in their place bars of iron. The covenant sign-act intensification: Hananyah breaking the wooden yoke does not remove the covenant judgment — it replaces a wooden yoke with an iron one. The covenant judgment is made heavier by the false prophetic resistance to it.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — as for the prophet who prophesies peace, when the word comes to pass, then it will be known that YHWH has truly sent the prophet. Yirmeyahu's covenant criterion for evaluating the peace prophet: wait and see. The test of fulfillment. The covenant community cannot always distinguish in the moment — but time is the ultimate covenant arbiter. Hananyah did not wait two years. He died in two months.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 29 The Letter to the Exiles — Build Houses and Plant Gardens — Seek the Welfare of the City — Plans for a Future and a Hope

1 These are the words of the letter that Yirmeyahu the prophet sent from Yerushalayim to the surviving elders of the exiles, and to the priests, the prophets, and all the people, whom Nevukhadnetzar had taken into exile from Yerushalayim to Babylon.

2 This was after King Yekhonyahu and the queen mother, the eunuchs, the officials of Yehudah and Yerushalayim, the craftsmen, and the metal workers had departed from Yerushalayim.

3 The letter was sent by the hand of El'asah the son of Shafan and Gemariyahu the son of Khilkiyahu, whom Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah sent to Babylon to Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon. It said:

4 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL, to all the exiles whom I have sent into exile from Yerushalayim to Babylon:

5 Build houses and live in them. Plant gardens and eat their produce.

6 Take wives and have sons and daughters. Take wives for your sons, and give your daughters in marriage, that they may bear sons and daughters. Multiply there, and do not decrease.

7 But seek the welfare of the city where I have sent you into exile, and pray to YHWH on its behalf, for in its welfare you will find your welfare.

8 For thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Do not let your prophets and your diviners who are among you deceive you, and do not listen to the dreams that they dream,

9 For it is a lie that they are prophesying to you in my name. I did not send them, declares YHWH.

10 For thus says YHWH: When seventy years are completed for Babylon, I will visit you, and I will fulfill to you my promise and bring you back to this place.

11 For I know the plans I have for you, declares YHWH, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope.

12 Then you will call upon me and come and pray to me, and I will hear you.

13 You will seek me and find me, when you seek me with all your heart.

14 I will be found by you, declares YHWH, and I will restore your fortunes and gather you from all the nations and all the places where I have driven you, declares YHWH, and I will bring you back to the place from which I sent you into exile.

15 Because you have said, YHWH has raised up prophets for us in Babylon,

16 Thus says YHWH concerning the king who sits on the throne of Dawid, and concerning all the people who dwell in this city, your kinsmen who did not go out with you into exile:

17 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Behold, I am sending on them sword, famine, and pestilence, and I will make them like vile figs that are so rotten they cannot be eaten.

18 I will pursue them with sword, famine, and pestilence, and will make them a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth, to be a curse, a terror, a hissing, and a reproach among all the nations where I have driven them,

19 Because they did not pay attention to my words, declares YHWH, that I persistently sent to them by my servants the prophets, but you would not listen, declares YHWH.

20 You, therefore, hear the word of YHWH, all you exiles whom I sent away from Yerushalayim to Babylon.

21 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL, concerning Akh'av the son of Kolayahu and Tzidkiyahu the son of Ma'aseiyahu, who are prophesying a lie to you in my name: Behold, I will deliver them into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, and he shall strike them down before your eyes.

22 Because of them this curse shall be used by all the exiles from Yehudah in Babylon: YHWH make you like Tzidkiyahu and Akh'av, whom the king of Babylon roasted in the fire,

23 Because they have done an outrageous thing in Yisra'EL, they have committed adultery with their neighbors' wives, and they have spoken in my name lying words that I did not command them. I am the one who knows, and I am witness, declares YHWH.

24 To Shemayahu of Nekhlam you shall say:

25 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: You have sent letters in your name to all the people who are in Yerushalayim, and to Tzefanyahu the son of Ma'aseiyahu the priest, and to all the priests, saying,

26 YHWH has made you priest instead of Yehoyada the priest, to have charge in the house of YHWH over every madman who prophesies, to put him in the stocks and neck irons.

27 Now why have you not rebuked Yirmeyahu of Anatot who is prophesying to you?

28 For he has sent to us in Babylon, saying, Your exile will be long — build houses and live in them, and plant gardens and eat their produce.

29 Tzefanyahu the priest read this letter in the hearing of Yirmeyahu the prophet.

30 Then the word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu:

31 Send to all the exiles, saying, Thus says YHWH concerning Shemayahu of Nekhlam: Because Shemayahu has prophesied to you when I did not send him, and has made you trust in a lie,

32 Therefore thus says YHWH: Behold, I will punish Shemayahu of Nekhlam and his offspring. He shall not have anyone living among this people, and he shall not see the good that I will do to my people, declares YHWH, for he has spoken rebellion against YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 29 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The letter to the exiles is the covenant document that defines the theology of diaspora existence: the covenant community in exile is still the covenant people — they are to build, plant, marry, multiply, and seek the welfare of the city where YHWH has placed them. Exile is not the end of covenant life — it is a covenant season.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 29 in full. Verse 11 — I know the plans I have for you, declares YHWH, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope — is the covenant declaration of purposeful exile. The deportation is not random catastrophe but covenant design toward a covenant goal.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 29. Verse 7 — seek the welfare of the city where I have sent you into exile, and pray to YHWH on its behalf — is the covenant counter-cultural command: pray for Babylon. The covenant community's welfare is bound to Babylon's welfare for seventy years. Covenant resistance to Babylon is not the calling — covenant witness within Babylon is.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — you will seek me and find me, when you seek me with all your heart. The covenant promise of findability: YHWH is not hidden from the covenant seeker. The exile does not remove YHWH's accessibility — it intensifies the covenant search. The community that is stripped of temple, land, and king discovers that YHWH can be found in Babylon as surely as in Yerushalayim. This is the covenant deconstruction of sacred geography.

THE BOOK OF CONSOLATION — CHAPTERS 30 AND 31 — THE NEW COVENANT

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 30 The Time of Yaakov's Trouble — I Will Save You From Afar — I Will Restore Your Fortunes — Your Wound Is Incurable

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH:

2 Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Write in a scroll all the words that I have spoken to you.

3 For behold, days are coming, declares YHWH, when I will restore the fortunes of my people, Yisra'EL and Yehudah, says YHWH, and I will bring them back to the land that I gave to their fathers, and they shall take possession of it.

4 These are the words that YHWH spoke concerning Yisra'EL and Yehudah:

5 Thus says YHWH: We have heard a cry of panic, of terror, and no peace.

6 Ask now, and see, can a man bear a child? Why then do I see every man with his hands on his stomach like a woman in labor? Why has every face turned pale?

7 Alas, that day is so great there is none like it. It is a time of distress for Yaakov, yet he shall be saved out of it.

8 And it shall come to pass in that day, declares YHWH of hosts, that I will break his yoke from off your neck, and I will burst your bonds, and foreigners shall no more make a servant of him.

9 But they shall serve YHWH their Creator and Dawid their king, whom I will raise up for them.

10 Then fear not, O Yaakov my servant, declares YHWH, nor be dismayed, O Yisra'EL. For behold, I will save you from far away, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Yaakov shall return and have quiet and ease, and none shall make him afraid.

11 For I am with you to save you, declares YHWH. I will make a full end of all the nations among whom I scattered you, but of you I will not make a full end. I will discipline you in just measure, and I will by no means leave you unpunished.

12 For thus says YHWH: Your hurt is incurable, and your wound is grievous.

13 There is none to uphold your cause, no medicine for your wound, no healing for you.

14 All your lovers have forgotten you — they care nothing for you. For I have dealt you the blow of an enemy, the punishment of a merciless foe, because your guilt is great, because your sins are flagrant.

15 Why do you cry out over your hurt? Your pain is incurable. Because your guilt is great, because your sins are flagrant, I have done these things to you.

16 Therefore all who devour you shall be devoured, and all your foes, every one of them, shall go into captivity. Those who plunder you shall be plundered, and all who prey on you I will make a prey.

17 For I will restore health to you, and your wounds I will heal, declares YHWH, because they have called you an outcast: It is Tziyon, for whom no one cares.

18 Thus says YHWH: Behold, I will restore the fortunes of the tents of Yaakov and have compassion on his dwellings. The city shall be rebuilt on its mound, and the palace shall stand where it used to be.

19 Out of them shall come songs of thanksgiving, and the voices of those who celebrate. I will multiply them, and they shall not be few. I will make them honored, and they shall not be small.

20 Their children shall be as they were of old, and their congregation shall be established before me, and I will punish all who oppress them.

21 Their prince shall be one of themselves — their ruler shall come out from their midst. I will make him draw near, and he shall approach me, for who would dare of himself to approach me? declares YHWH.

22 And you shall be my people, and I will be your Creator.

23 Behold the storm of YHWH — wrath has gone forth, a whirling tempest. It will burst upon the head of the wicked.

24 The fierce anger of YHWH will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intentions of his heart. In the latter days you will understand this.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 30 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 30 opens what scholars call the Book of Consolation — Chapters 30-33 — the covenant restoration promises at the heart of the entire book. After twenty-nine chapters of covenant judgment, the covenant direction reverses: YHWH announces the restoration he will accomplish.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 30 in full. Verse 7 — a time of distress for Yaakov, yet he shall be saved out of it — is the covenant declaration that defines the structure of the entire prophetic message: the covenant tribulation is real and severe, but it does not have the final word. The covenant salvation comes through the tribulation, not around it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 30. Verse 17 — I will restore health to you, and your wounds I will heal, declares YHWH, because they have called you an outcast. The covenant reversal of the incurable wound: what no human medicine can cure, YHWH heals. The covenant wound that verse 12-13 declares incurable is healed by the covenant declaration of verse 17.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 21 — their prince shall be one of themselves — their ruler shall come out from their midst. The covenant restoration includes covenant leadership: a ruler from within the covenant community, not a foreign imposed sovereign. The covenant formula of verse 22 — you shall be my people, and I will be your Creator — is the Sinai covenant formula that has been violated throughout the book, now restored as the goal of the covenant restoration.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 31 I Have Loved You With an Everlasting Love — Rachel Weeping for Her Children — The New Covenant — I Will Write My Torah on Their Hearts

1 At that time, declares YHWH, I will be the Creator of all the clans of Yisra'EL, and they shall be my people.

2 Thus says YHWH: The people who survived the sword found grace in the wilderness — when Yisra'EL sought for rest,

3 YHWH appeared to him from far away. I have loved you with an everlasting love — therefore I have continued my faithfulness to you.

4 Again I will build you, and you shall be built, O virgin Yisra'EL. Again you shall adorn yourself with tambourines and shall go forth in the dance of the merrymakers.

5 Again you shall plant vineyards on the mountains of Samaria — the planters shall plant and shall enjoy the fruit.

6 For there shall be a day when watchmen will call in the hill country of Efrayim: Arise, and let us go up to Tziyon, to YHWH our Creator.

7 For thus says YHWH: Sing aloud with gladness for Yaakov, and shout for the chief of the nations. Proclaim, give praise, and say, O YHWH, save your people, the remnant of Yisra'EL.

8 Behold, I will bring them from the north country and gather them from the farthest parts of the earth, among them the blind and the lame, the pregnant woman and she who is in labor, together. A great company, they shall return here.

9 With weeping they shall come, and with pleas for mercy I will lead them back. I will make them walk by brooks of water, in a straight path in which they shall not stumble, for I am a father to Yisra'EL, and Efrayim is my firstborn.

10 Hear the word of YHWH, O nations, and declare it in the coastlands far away. Say, He who scattered Yisra'EL will gather him, and will keep him as a shepherd keeps his flock.

11 For YHWH has ransomed Yaakov and has redeemed him from hands too strong for him.

12 They shall come and sing aloud on the height of Tziyon, and they shall be radiant over the goodness of YHWH — over the grain, the wine, and the oil, and over the young of the flock and the herd. Their life shall be like a watered garden, and they shall languish no more.

13 Then shall the young women rejoice in the dance, and the young men and the old shall be merry. I will turn their mourning into joy — I will comfort them, and give them gladness for sorrow.

14 I will feast the soul of the priests with abundance, and my people shall be satisfied with my goodness, declares YHWH.

15 Thus says YHWH: A voice is heard in Ramah, lamentation and bitter weeping. Rachel is weeping for her children — she refuses to be comforted for her children, because they are no more.

16 Thus says YHWH: Keep your voice from weeping, and your eyes from tears, for there is a reward for your work, declares YHWH, and they shall come back from the land of the enemy.

17 There is hope for your future, declares YHWH, and your children shall come back to their own country.

18 I have heard Efrayim grieving: You have disciplined me, and I was disciplined, like an untrained calf. Bring me back that I may be restored, for you are YHWH my Creator.

19 For after I had turned away, I relented, and after I was instructed, I struck my thigh. I was ashamed, and I was confounded, because I bore the disgrace of my youth.

20 Is Efrayim my dear son? Is he my darling child? For as often as I speak against him, I do remember him still. Therefore my heart yearns for him — I will surely have mercy on him, declares YHWH.

21 Set up road markers for yourself — make yourself guideposts. Consider well the highway, the road by which you went. Return, O virgin Yisra'EL, return to these your cities.

22 How long will you waver, O faithless daughter? For YHWH has created a new thing on the earth: a woman encircles a man.

23 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Once more they shall use these words in the land of Yehudah and in its cities, when I restore their fortunes: YHWH bless you, O habitation of righteousness, O holy hill.

24 And Yehudah and all its cities shall dwell there together, and the farmers and those who wander with their flocks.

25 For I will satisfy the weary soul, and every languishing soul I will replenish.

26 At this I awoke and looked, and my sleep was pleasant to me.

27 Behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when I will sow the house of Yisra'EL and the house of Yehudah with the seed of man and the seed of beast.

28 And it shall come to pass that as I have watched over them to pluck up and break down, to overthrow, destroy, and bring harm, so I will watch over them to build and to plant, declares YHWH.

29 In those days they shall no longer say, The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge.

30 But everyone shall die for his own iniquity. Each man who eats sour grapes, his teeth shall be set on edge.

31 Behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Yisra'EL and the house of Yehudah,

32 Not like the covenant that I made with their fathers on the day when I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt, my covenant that they broke, though I was their husband, declares YHWH.

33 For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Yisra'EL after those days, declares YHWH: I will put my Torah within them, and I will write it on their hearts. And I will be their Creator, and they shall be my people.

34 And no longer shall each one teach his neighbor and each his brother, saying, Know YHWH, for they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest, declares YHWH. For I will forgive their iniquity, and I will remember their sin no more.

35 Thus says YHWH, who gives the sun for light by day and the fixed order of the moon and the stars for light by night, who stirs up the sea so that its waves roar — YHWH of hosts is his name:

36 If this fixed order departs from before me, declares YHWH, then shall the offspring of Yisra'EL cease from being a nation before me forever.

37 Thus says YHWH: If the heavens above can be measured, and the foundations of the earth below can be explored, then I will cast off all the offspring of Yisra'EL for all that they have done, declares YHWH.

38 Behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when the city shall be rebuilt for YHWH from the Tower of Hanan'el to the Corner Gate.

39 And the measuring line shall go out farther, straight to the hill Garev, and shall then turn to Go'ah.

40 The whole valley of the dead bodies and the ashes, and all the fields as far as the brook Kidron, to the corner of the Horse Gate toward the east, shall be sacred to YHWH. It shall not be uprooted or overthrown anymore forever.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-b preserves significant portions of Chapter 31. The new covenant declaration of verses 31-34 is the theological summit of the entire book and one of the most consequential covenant texts in all of the Hebrew prophetic corpus. The DSS preserves this passage in close agreement with the Masoretic. The new covenant is not a replacement of the Sinai covenant but its internalized fulfillment: the same Torah, written on the heart rather than stone.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 31 in full. Verses 33-34 — I will put my Torah within them, and I will write it on their hearts — and no longer shall each one teach his neighbor, saying Know YHWH, for they shall all know me — is read as the covenant declaration that the crisis of Chapters 1-29 — the heart of deception, the death of covenant knowledge — is resolved by YHWH's sovereign act of covenant inscription.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 31. Verse 15 — Rachel weeping for her children — she refuses to be comforted because they are no more — is the covenant image of the matriarch Rachel, buried near Ramah, weeping over the northern exiles who passed her tomb. The covenant response — verse 16-17 — is the only covenant word that can address covenant grief of this depth: there is a reward for your work, and they shall come back.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31-34 — the new covenant. YHWH declares a new covenant with the house of Yisra'EL and the house of Yehudah — not like the Sinai covenant that the people broke. The new covenant has the same Torah — same content, same covenant Creator-people formula — but a different medium. Written on stone, the Torah remained external and resistible. Written on the heart, the Torah becomes the internal covenant reality of the community. Verse 34 — they shall all know me, from the least to the greatest. The covenant knowledge that has been absent throughout the book — my people know me not, Chapter 2:8; they do not know me, Chapter 9:3 — is now given by YHWH himself as a covenant gift. The new covenant resolves the fundamental covenant problem the book diagnoses.

END OF PART FOUR — CHAPTERS 24 THROUGH 31
